walkthrough.title: Place the Game Board
walkthrough.text: Let's get Fury of Dracula set up. First, unfold the large game board showing the map of Europe and place it in the center of the table where everyone can reach it. The board shows cities connected by roads and railways, sea zones along the coasts, and a trail area along one side. The player controlling Dracula should sit near the trail side of the board.

walkthrough.title: Set the Influence Track
walkthrough.text: Find the influence marker, which is a small token used to track Dracula's growing power. Place it on space zero of the influence track on the board. This track goes from zero to thirteen. If Dracula ever reaches thirteen, he wins the game. The hunters need to defeat Dracula before that happens.

walkthrough.title: Set the Time Track
walkthrough.text: Now find the time marker and place it on the yellow Monday day space on the time track. The time track alternates between day and night spaces and cycles through the days of the week. Each round, the time marker advances through dawn and dusk. When it passes from Sunday night to Monday day, a full week has passed, and you place a despair token on the time track.

walkthrough.title: Assign Roles
walkthrough.text: One player chooses to control Dracula. That player should sit near the trail side of the board. The remaining players each choose one of the four hunters to control: Lord Godalming, Dr. John Seward, Van Helsing, or Mina Harker. Each player takes the matching character sheet and plastic figure. Each hunter player also takes one hunter reference card. Dracula takes the reference map. If you have fewer than five players, some players will control multiple hunters. With three hunter players, one controls two hunters. With two hunter players, each controls two. With one hunter player, that person controls all four.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Hunter Card Decks
walkthrough.text: Now set up the hunter cards. Shuffle all 38 item cards into a face-down deck and place it near the board. Then take all 75 event cards, which include both hunter event cards and Dracula event cards, shuffle them together into one single deck and place it near the item deck. Finally, sort the 12 hunter combat cards by type into three distinct piles: Dodge, Punch, and Escape. Place these piles near the item deck where all hunters can reach them.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Dracula Card Decks
walkthrough.text: Sort the 28 encounter cards into one deck and shuffle it. Sort the 13 Dracula combat cards into a separate deck and shuffle it. Then create the location deck by organizing all 70 location cards numerically. There are 60 land location cards and 10 sea location cards. Place all three decks face-down near Dracula's side of the board.

walkthrough.title: Create the Token Pool
walkthrough.text: Place the 16 ticket tokens face-down near the board and randomize them. Then separate all remaining tokens by type and place them in piles near the board. You should have piles for: 31 damage tokens (seven showing value 5 and twenty-four showing value 1), 6 storm tokens, 4 fog tokens, 2 bats tokens, 3 despair tokens, 4 bite tokens, 8 roadblock tokens, 1 consecrated ground token, 3 heavenly host tokens, and 4 rumor tokens.

walkthrough.title: Dracula Draws Encounter Cards
walkthrough.text: Dracula draws five encounter cards from the encounter deck to form his starting hand. He keeps these hidden from the hunters. These are the cards Dracula will place on the trail as he moves, representing traps, vampires, and other dangers for the hunters to discover.

walkthrough.title: Place the Hunters
walkthrough.text: Each hunter places their plastic figure on their designated starting city on the board. Lord Godalming starts in Constanta, Dr. John Seward starts in Marseilles, Mina Harker starts in Brussels, and Van Helsing starts in Amsterdam. In the advanced game, hunters can choose their own starting cities instead.

walkthrough.title: Choose Dracula's Starting Location
walkthrough.text: Finally, Dracula secretly chooses his starting location. He searches through the location deck, finds the card matching his chosen city, and places it face-down on the first space of the trail, which is the leftmost space. Dracula cannot start on a sea zone, Castle Dracula, or any city that a hunter currently occupies. After placing the card, Dracula puts his figure on the red circle near the first trail space. The hunters do not know where Dracula started. The game is ready to begin.

Dracula player sits near the trail side

bullets: Place influence marker on space 0
Track goes from 0 to 13 — Dracula wins at 13

summary.title: Set the Time Track
bullets: Place time marker on yellow Monday (day) space
Track alternates day/night, cycles through the week
Place 1 despair token each time a full week passes

Each player takes matching character sheet, figure, and reference card
Fewer than 5 players: some control multiple hunters

Shuffle all 75 event cards (hunter + Dracula) into one deck
Sort 12 hunter combat cards into 3 piles: Dodge, Punch, Escape

Shuffle 13 Dracula combat cards into a separate deck
Organize 70 location cards (60 land + 10 sea) numerically

31 damage tokens (7x '5' + 24x '1')
6 storm, 4 fog, 2 bats, 3 despair, 4 bite tokens
8 roadblock, 1 consecrated ground, 3 heavenly host, 4 rumor tokens

summary.title: Dracula Draws Encounter Cards
bullets: Dracula draws 5 encounter cards as starting hand
Keep hidden from all hunters

summary.title: Place the Hunters
bullets: Lord Godalming in Constanta
Dr. Seward in Marseilles
Mina Harker in Brussels
Van Helsing in Amsterdam
Advanced game: hunters choose starting cities

summary.title: Choose Dracula's Starting Location
bullets: Dracula secretly picks a city from the location deck
Places location card face-down on trail space 1
Cannot start on sea zone, Castle Dracula, or hunter-occupied city
Places figure on red circle near trail — game begins

walkthrough.title: Game Structure: Rounds and Phases
walkthrough.text: Fury of Dracula is played over a series of rounds. Each round has two phases: the hunter phase, then the Dracula phase. During the hunter phase, all four hunters take actions during both day and night. During the Dracula phase, Dracula moves secretly and places encounter cards. Rounds continue until one side wins. Hunters act in activation order, which is the number printed on the upper-left corner of each character sheet.

walkthrough.title: The Hunter Phase: Day and Night
walkthrough.text: Each hunter phase contains both day and night. First, dawn occurs, during which the time marker advances from night to day and any combat with a revealed Dracula takes place. Then each hunter performs one action during day, in activation order. After all day actions, dusk occurs, during which the time marker advances from day to night and any combat with a revealed Dracula occurs. Then each hunter performs one night action. Hunters in sea zones must move during day and must pass during night.

walkthrough.title: Hunter Actions Overview
walkthrough.text: There are seven actions a hunter can perform during each day or night period. Move lets you travel by road, railway, or sea. Supply lets you draw item and event cards. Search lets you reveal encounter cards at your location. Rest lets you recover one damage. Trade lets you exchange item cards and ticket tokens with another hunter in your city. Reserve a Ticket gives you a ticket token for railway travel. Special lets you use an ability from your character sheet or an event card. Not all actions are available in all situations. Only move actions can be taken during day when in a sea zone.

walkthrough.title: Movement by Road, Railway, and Sea
walkthrough.text: When moving by road, a hunter moves from one city to an adjacent city connected by a road line on the board. Moving by railway requires spending a ticket token. The ticket token has a white number and sometimes a yellow number indicating how many railway connections you can use. White railways use the white value, and any combination involving yellow railways uses the yellow value. Moving by sea is only allowed during day. A hunter in a port can move to an adjacent sea zone, and a hunter in a sea zone can move to an adjacent port or sea zone. If a hunter is at sea during day, they must move. If at sea during night, they must pass.

walkthrough.title: The Supply Action
walkthrough.text: When a hunter performs a supply action in a large city, they draw the top item card from the item deck and place it in their hand. In a small city, they do not draw an item card. Then, an event card is drawn. During day, the hunter takes the top card of the event deck. If it has a hunter icon, the hunter keeps it. If it has a Dracula icon, it is discarded face-down. During night, the hunter takes the bottom card of the event deck. If it has a hunter icon, the hunter keeps it. If it has a Dracula icon, Dracula draws it instead. Lord Godalming's Wealth ability lets him draw two item cards instead of one when supplying in a large city.

walkthrough.title: Search, Rest, Trade, Reserve a Ticket, and Special Actions
walkthrough.text: Search: If your city has encounter cards on the trail, you may reveal and resolve all encounter card effects at your location, ignoring matured effects. Rest: Recover one damage. Dr. Seward's Physician ability makes rest recover two damage instead. Trade: Exchange item cards and ticket tokens privately with another hunter in your city. Van Helsing's Leader ability lets him trade event cards with a hunter in any city. Reserve a Ticket: Gain one ticket token from the pool. Lord Godalming's Privilege ability gives two tickets instead of one. Special: Use an ability from your character sheet or an event card that specifies it can be used as an action. Mina Harker's Psychic Bond lets her force Dracula to reveal if he is in her region.

walkthrough.title: The Dracula Phase: Movement
walkthrough.text: After all hunters finish their night actions, the Dracula phase begins. Dracula moves secretly by placing location cards face-down on the trail. First, all existing cards on the trail slide one space to the right. Then Dracula picks a location card from his location deck matching a city adjacent to his current location by road, and places it face-down on the first trail space. Dracula cannot move by railway. Sea location cards have a different card back, alerting hunters when Dracula moves to sea. Moving from a port to a sea zone costs Dracula two damage. Moving from one sea zone to another costs one damage. Dracula cannot place a location card that is already on the trail.

walkthrough.title: The Dracula Phase: Encounter Cards and the Trail
walkthrough.text: After moving, Dracula places one encounter card from his hand face-down on top of the location card on the first trail space. If Dracula moved to a sea zone or is currently revealed, he cannot place an encounter card. If Dracula has fewer than five encounter cards, he draws until he has five. The trail has six spaces. Each space holds a hideout, which consists of a location card and any encounter cards or tokens on it. If there are already six hideouts when Dracula places a new card, the sixth hideout matures and slides off.

walkthrough.title: Maturing Encounters and Vampires
walkthrough.text: When a hideout slides off the sixth space of the trail, Dracula may resolve the matured effect printed on that encounter card, then discards it. The location card returns to the location deck. Vampire encounter cards are especially important. There are three types: New Vampire, Aristocratic Vampire, and Reckless Vampire. When a vampire encounter matures, its matured effect advances the influence track, bringing Dracula closer to victory. The matured effect also instructs Dracula to clear specific hideouts on the trail. This is one of Dracula's primary paths to winning.

walkthrough.title: Finding the Trail and Ambushing
walkthrough.text: When a hunter moves into a city, Dracula must check if that city appears as a location card anywhere on the trail. If it does, that location card is flipped face-up, revealing it. If a hunter ends movement on a hideout with encounter cards, Dracula may choose to ambush by revealing and resolving one encounter card, ignoring the matured effect. If Dracula does not ambush, the encounter card stays and the hunter can later use a search action to reveal it. If a hunter moves to Dracula's current location, the first trail space, Dracula is revealed and his figure is placed on the board. Combat occurs at the next dawn or dusk.

walkthrough.title: Combat Basics
walkthrough.text: Combat occurs when a hunter is in the same city as Dracula at dawn or dusk. The hunter takes one each of Dodge, Punch, and Escape from the combat card piles and adds them to their hand. Dracula shuffles his combat deck and draws five cards. Each combat round follows six steps. Both sides secretly choose a card, then reveal simultaneously. Check if the combat icon on Dracula's card matches a highlighted icon on the hunter's banner. If it matches, Dracula's effect is canceled. Then resolve Dracula's effect if not canceled, then resolve the hunter's effect. Finally, Dracula draws a new combat card, and the hunter retrieves their previously played card.

walkthrough.title: Combat End Conditions and Multiple Hunters
walkthrough.text: Combat ends when any of these conditions are met: Dracula has played six combat cards, Dracula wins by reaching influence 13, the hunters win by defeating Dracula, all hunters in the combat are bitten, defeated, or escaped, or Dracula resolves Escape as Bat or Escape as Mist. Dracula's Pride ability restricts escape cards: he cannot play them until he has played a number of combat cards greater than the number of despair tokens on the time track. When multiple hunters are in combat, each chooses a card, but Dracula picks one engaged hunter whose card can block his. Both hunters' card effects resolve.

walkthrough.title: Damage, Bites, and Defeat
walkthrough.text: When a character suffers damage, place damage tokens on their character sheet equal to the amount. If the total damage equals or exceeds their health value, they are defeated. When a hunter is bitten, place a bite token on a bite space on their sheet. Most hunters have one bite space and are defeated by a second bite. Van Helsing has two bite spaces. Mina Harker starts with a permanent bite and is defeated by her first additional bite. A weakened hunter with bite tokens must keep one item card and one event card revealed at all times. When a hunter is defeated, the influence track advances by two plus the number of despair tokens. The hunter is placed at the nearest hospital at dawn and discards all cards and tokens.

walkthrough.title: The Time Track and Despair
walkthrough.text: The time track cycles through days of the week. At dawn the marker advances from night to day; at dusk it advances from day to night. When the marker moves from Sunday night to Monday day, a week has passed. Place one despair token on the time track. There are three despair token spaces. Despair tokens increase the influence gained when hunters are defeated. After all three despair tokens are placed, Dracula's Fury of Dracula ability activates: each time he places a location card or Hide on the trail, the influence track advances by three. This creates extreme urgency for the hunters in the late game.

walkthrough.title: Event and Item Cards
walkthrough.text: Event cards have timing text at the top describing when they can be played. Some must be played immediately, others can be saved. Some event cards can be played as allies and are placed in the ally slot on the board. Only one hunter ally and one Dracula ally can be in play at a time. A new ally replaces the old one. Item cards are mostly used during combat and have colored banners. Cards without banners describe their own timing. Hunters have a hand limit of three for each card type, shown on their character sheet. Dr. Seward's Prepared ability raises his limit to four of each. Dracula has a hand limit of four event cards.

walkthrough.title: Tokens on the Board
walkthrough.text: Several token types can be placed on the board by encounter or event cards. Fog tokens prevent hunters from searching, entering, leaving, or passing through a city. One fog token is removed from each city at each dusk. Bats tokens are placed under hunter figures; Dracula can move that hunter to an adjacent city as their next action, then the token is removed. Roadblock tokens block a road or railway. One is removed at each dusk. Storm tokens prevent hunters from entering a sea zone. One is removed at each dusk. Consecrated ground and heavenly host tokens reveal the location and its encounters, and Dracula cannot enter that location. Heavenly host tokens are removed at dawn.

walkthrough.title: Special Locations
walkthrough.text: Castle Dracula has a unique card back so hunters know when it is played. When Dracula places it on the trail, he heals five damage. If a hunter is at Castle Dracula, they cannot supply or reserve a ticket. Hospitals are in Madrid, Rome, and Budapest. When a hunter is defeated, they are placed at the nearest hospital at dawn, measured by roads. At a hospital, a hunter can only supply to draw item cards, and during day can move to the attached city.

walkthrough.title: Advanced Rules: Power Cards and Lairs
walkthrough.text: After your first game, add the advanced rules. Dracula gains five power cards: Feed lets him recover up to three damage instead of moving. Dark Call costs two damage but draws five encounter cards. Wolf Form costs one damage but lets Dracula move up to two cities away by road, passing through cities undetected. Misdirect clears a hideout on the trail. Hide lets Dracula stay in his current location while pretending to move, placing a face-down card that looks like a location card. Dracula can also convert hideouts sliding off the trail into lairs instead of maturing them, storing them with additional encounter cards in lair slots for future use.

walkthrough.title: Winning the Game
walkthrough.text: Dracula wins by advancing the influence track to space 13. He gains influence by maturing vampire encounters, resolving the Fangs combat card against a mesmerized hunter which advances the track by one, defeating hunters which advances the track by two plus the number of despair tokens, and through his Fury of Dracula ability after all three despair tokens are placed. Rumor tokens on maturing vampire hideouts add three additional influence. The hunters win if Dracula accumulates 15 damage tokens on his character sheet, thereby defeating him. There is no other hunter victory condition. The hunters must find and destroy Dracula.

bullets: Each round: Hunter Phase then Dracula Phase
Hunters act in activation order (printed on character sheets)
Rounds continue until one side wins

bullets: Dawn: advance time marker, fight revealed Dracula
Day: each hunter performs 1 action in activation order
Dusk: advance time marker, fight revealed Dracula
Night: each hunter performs 1 action in activation order

bullets: Move (road/railway/sea), Supply (draw cards), Search (reveal encounters)
Rest (recover 1 damage), Trade (exchange cards with nearby hunter)
Reserve a Ticket (gain ticket token), Special (character sheet/event ability)
Sea zones: must move during day, must pass during night

Railway: spend a ticket token, move up to its value in railway connections
White value for white railways, yellow value for any yellow railways
Sea: port to adjacent sea zone, or sea zone to adjacent port/sea zone (day only)

summary.title: The Supply Action
bullets: Large city: draw 1 item card (Lord Godalming draws 2)
Day: take top event card (hunter icon = keep, Dracula icon = discard)
Night: take bottom event card (hunter icon = keep, Dracula icon = Dracula draws)
Small city: no item card, still draw event card

Rest: recover 1 damage (Dr. Seward recovers 2)

Reserve a Ticket: gain 1 ticket (Lord Godalming gains 2)

bullets: Slide all trail cards one space right
Place a location card face-down on trail space 1

Port to sea = 2 damage; sea to sea = 1 damage

bullets: Place 1 encounter card face-down on trail space 1 (after moving)
Cannot place if on sea or currently revealed
Draw encounter cards to refill hand to 5
Trail has 6 spaces; 6th hideout matures and slides off

summary.title: Maturing Encounters and Vampires
bullets: Matured hideout: resolve matured effect, discard encounter card, return location
Vampire encounters (New, Aristocratic, Reckless) advance influence track when matured
Vampires maturing is Dracula's primary path to victory
Matured vampires also clear specific hideouts on the trail

Search action reveals all encounter cards at hunter's location

Matching icon on hunter's banner cancels Dracula's combat card effect

Multiple hunters: each chooses a card, Dracula picks one engaged hunter to block

Bite token on bite space; weakened hunters must reveal 1 item + 1 event card
Most hunters: 1 bite space (defeated on 2nd bite); Van Helsing: 2 bite spaces

Sunday night to Monday day = 1 week passed, place 1 despair token
3 despair tokens total; increase influence gain when hunters defeated

Item cards mostly used in combat (have colored banners)

Bats: Dracula moves hunter to adjacent city on next action; then removed
Roadblock: blocks road or railway; removed 1 at dusk
Storm: blocks sea zone entry; removed 1 at dusk
Consecrated ground / Heavenly host: reveal location, block Dracula (host removed at dawn)

Hunters at Castle Dracula cannot supply or reserve a ticket
Hospitals (Madrid, Rome, Budapest): defeated hunters placed at nearest hospital at dawn
At hospital: can supply for items only; move to attached city during day

bullets: Feed: recover 3 damage (no move). Dark Call: suffer 2 damage, draw 5 encounters
Wolf Form: suffer 1 damage, move up to 2 cities by road. Misdirect: clear a hideout
Hide: stay in place, place face-down card that looks like a location card
Lairs: convert maturing hideouts to lair slots (up to 3), add encounter cards, revisit later

summary.title: Winning the Game
bullets: Dracula wins: advance influence track to 13
Sources: mature vampires, defeat hunters (2 + despair tokens), Fangs on mesmerized hunter, Fury ability (+3)
Hunters win: deal 15 total damage to Dracula
Rumor tokens on maturing vampires add +3 influence

walkthrough.title: Welcome & Meet the Players
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through a scripted first round with five players. Player 1 controls Dracula, while Players 2 through 5 control the four hunters: Lord Godalming, Dr. Seward, Van Helsing, and Mina Harker. This is the recommended player count for your first game. Follow along with the moves, even if you have fewer players. Ready? Let's hunt the vampire.

walkthrough.title: Setup Complete — The Hunt Begins
walkthrough.text: The board is set up. Lord Godalming is in Constanta, Dr. Seward is in Marseilles, Van Helsing is in Amsterdam, and Mina Harker is in Brussels. Dracula has secretly chosen his starting city and placed a face-down location card on the first trail space. Nobody knows where the Count is hiding. The time marker is on Monday day.

walkthrough.title: Dawn — Start of the Hunter Phase
walkthrough.text: The first thing that happens each hunter phase is dawn. At dawn, advance the time marker. Since this is the very first round, the marker is already on Monday day, so we simply note it. There is no revealed Dracula, so no dawn combat occurs. Now the hunters will each take their day action.

walkthrough.title: Lord Godalming's Day Action: Move
walkthrough.text: Lord Godalming goes first based on activation order. He is in Constanta, in the east of Europe. He decides to move by road toward the center of the map to start covering ground. He moves to Bucharest, which is adjacent to Constanta by road. He places his figure on Bucharest. Dracula checks the trail and Bucharest is not on it, so nothing happens.

walkthrough.title: Dr. Seward's Day Action: Supply
walkthrough.text: Dr. Seward is in Marseilles, a large city. He performs a supply action. Since it is a large city, he draws one item card from the item deck and adds it to his hand. Then he draws the top card of the event deck. It has a hunter icon, so he keeps it. Good start. He now has one item card and one event card.

walkthrough.title: Van Helsing's Day Action: Reserve a Ticket
walkthrough.text: Van Helsing is in Amsterdam. He decides to prepare for fast travel by reserving a ticket. He takes one ticket token from the pool, looks at it secretly, and places it in his play area. Ticket tokens let hunters move by railway, which covers much more ground than road movement. Smart preparation.

walkthrough.title: Mina Harker's Day Action: Move
walkthrough.text: Mina Harker is in Brussels. She decides to move by road toward central Europe. She moves to Paris, which is adjacent to Brussels. Dracula checks the trail. Paris is not on the trail, so nothing is revealed. The hunters are spreading out across Europe, trying to cover as many regions as possible.

walkthrough.title: Dusk — Transition to Night
walkthrough.text: All day actions are complete. Now dusk occurs. The time marker advances from Monday day to Monday night. Dracula is not revealed, so there is no dusk combat. Now each hunter will take a night action.

walkthrough.title: Lord Godalming's Night Action: Supply
walkthrough.text: Lord Godalming is in Bucharest, a large city. He supplies during night. He draws one item card. Then he draws the bottom card of the event deck. It has a Dracula icon, which means Dracula gets to draw this card instead of Lord Godalming. Dracula smiles and adds it to his hand. This is the risk of night supply: Dracula might get the event card.

walkthrough.title: Dr. Seward's Night Action: The Mistake
walkthrough.text: Dr. Seward decides to rest, even though he has no damage. He recovers zero damage because he was already at full health. This was a wasted action. Rest only removes damage tokens, and Seward has taken none yet. He should have used this night action to supply, reserve a ticket, or move instead. Night actions are precious and should never be wasted.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Never Waste an Action
walkthrough.text: Every action matters in Fury of Dracula. The hunters are in a race against the clock. Dracula gains influence every round that his vampires mature, and the despair tokens make him stronger over time. Resting when you have no damage is like skipping a turn. Use night actions to supply in large cities, reserve tickets for fast travel later, or reposition. The only time you should rest is when you actually have damage to recover.

walkthrough.title: Van Helsing's Night Action: Supply
walkthrough.text: Van Helsing is in Amsterdam, a large city. He supplies during night. He draws one item card. Then he draws the bottom card of the event deck. This one has a hunter icon, so Van Helsing keeps it. Now he has a ticket token, an item card, and an event card. He is well-equipped for the days ahead.

walkthrough.title: Mina Harker's Night Action: Supply
walkthrough.text: Mina is in Paris, a large city. She supplies. She draws one item card and takes the bottom event card. It has a hunter icon, so she keeps it. Remember that Mina is always weakened because of her Drained ability. She starts with a permanent bite token and must always keep one item card and one event card revealed. She places one card of each type face-up in her card slots. The other hunters and Dracula can now see those cards.

walkthrough.title: The Dracula Phase Begins
walkthrough.text: All hunter actions are done. Now it is the Dracula phase. Dracula slides any existing hideouts on the trail one space to the right. Since this is only the second Dracula phase, there is just the starting location card, which slides from space one to space two. Now Dracula secretly chooses a city adjacent to his current location by road and places that location card face-down on space one. The hunters watch carefully. Is it a city card or a sea card? City card. That narrows Dracula's possible location, but only slightly.

walkthrough.title: Dracula Places an Encounter Card
walkthrough.text: After moving, Dracula selects an encounter card from his hand and places it face-down on top of his location card on trail space one. This could be a trap for the hunters or a new vampire. The hunters will not know what it is until they find this location on the trail and either get ambushed or use a search action. Dracula then checks his hand. If he has fewer than five encounter cards, he draws back up to five. The round is complete.

walkthrough.title: Mistake: Mina Moves to the Trail
walkthrough.text: In the next round, the hunters continue moving and supplying. During day, Mina decides to move by road from Paris to Bordeaux. Dracula checks the trail. Bordeaux is on trail space two. Dracula reveals that location card. Mina has found the trail. But there is an encounter card there, and Dracula chooses to ambush. He reveals the encounter card, which is Unnatural Fog. Fog tokens are placed on Bordeaux. Now Mina cannot search here and cannot easily leave. Mina was not prepared for this. She has no useful items to deal with the fog.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Prepare Before Approaching the Trail
walkthrough.text: The takeaway: when you find a location on Dracula's trail, you need to be ready for what you find there. Encounters can be traps, fog, bats, or vampires. Always have some item cards and event cards in hand before you go searching for Dracula. Charging in unprepared can waste several turns dealing with Dracula's tricks. Coordinate with other hunters to approach trail locations from multiple directions with good equipment.

walkthrough.title: Mistake: Hunters Ignore the Clock
walkthrough.text: By the end of the second full week, two despair tokens are on the time track. Dracula has been carefully planting vampires on the trail, and one is about to mature. The hunters have been wandering around gathering supplies without coordinating their search. A vampire encounter matures and Dracula advances the influence track by several spaces. He is now at influence 7. The hunters suddenly realize they are halfway to losing and have not even found Dracula's general region.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Always Track the Clock
walkthrough.text: The time track is your most important strategic indicator. Each despair token makes Dracula stronger. You need to be actively narrowing down Dracula's location from the start, not just gathering supplies. Spread out in the early game to cover different regions. When you find a trail location, communicate with other hunters to converge. Use Mina's Psychic Bond to check regions. Every round you waste is a round Dracula is placing vampires and building influence.

walkthrough.title: The Hunters Converge
walkthrough.text: Now the hunters get organized. Van Helsing uses his ticket token to move by railway across multiple cities in one action, rapidly closing the distance. Lord Godalming uses his Wealth ability to stock up on items in a large city. Dr. Seward positions himself along a likely escape route. The hunters communicate openly about which regions they have cleared and where Dracula might be. They are tightening the net.

walkthrough.title: First Combat with Dracula
walkthrough.text: Lord Godalming moves into a city and Dracula reveals it is his current location. Combat will occur at the next dusk. Lord Godalming takes the three hunter combat cards: Dodge, Punch, and Escape. He adds them to his item cards. Dracula shuffles and draws five combat cards. Round one of combat: Lord Godalming plays Punch, Dracula plays Claws. The icons do not match, so Claws resolves, dealing two damage to Lord Godalming. Then Punch resolves, dealing one damage to Dracula. The fight is on.

walkthrough.title: Continue the Round
walkthrough.text: The game continues with rounds of hunter phases and Dracula phases. Each round, the hunters take two actions each, day and night. Dracula moves and places encounter cards. The time marker advances, eventually placing despair tokens. The key rhythm is: hunters act strategically to narrow Dracula's location, equip themselves for combat, and converge when they find the trail. Dracula tries to evade, plant vampires, and run out the clock. Keep playing until one side wins.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: You have seen the core rhythm of Fury of Dracula: hunters spreading out, finding the trail, dealing with encounters, and engaging Dracula in combat. You have watched the hunters learn from three common mistakes: wasting actions, approaching the trail unprepared, and ignoring the clock. Remember: every action counts, always carry supplies, and track Dracula's influence carefully. You are ready to play a full game.

walkthrough.title: Before You Begin the Hunt
walkthrough.text:
Before you dive into a full game, here are three things that can help.

First, check out General Tips for beginner strategy on both sides of the table.

Second, reference the Appendix for a quick guide to encounter cards, combat cards, tokens, and special terms.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Good hunting.

summary.title: Welcome & Meet the Players
bullets: Scripted tutorial with 5 players: 1 Dracula + 4 hunters
Recommended player count for first game
Covers a full round from setup through the Dracula phase

summary.title: Setup Complete — The Hunt Begins
bullets: Hunters at starting cities: Constanta, Marseilles, Amsterdam, Brussels
Dracula's starting location is secret (face-down on trail)
Time marker on Monday day

summary.title: Dawn — Start of the Hunter Phase
bullets: Advance time marker at dawn (night to day)
No revealed Dracula = no dawn combat
Hunters prepare for day actions

summary.title: Lord Godalming's Day Action: Move
bullets: Moves by road from Constanta to Bucharest
Dracula checks trail — Bucharest not on it, nothing revealed
Goal: cover ground toward center of Europe

summary.title: Dr. Seward's Day Action: Supply
bullets: Marseilles is a large city — draws 1 item card
Draws top event card (hunter icon = keeps it)
Builds up supplies for later encounters

summary.title: Van Helsing's Day Action: Reserve a Ticket
bullets: Takes 1 ticket token from pool, views it secretly
Ticket tokens enable fast railway movement
Preparing for rapid repositioning later

summary.title: Mina Harker's Day Action: Move
bullets: Moves by road from Brussels to Paris
Paris not on Dracula's trail — nothing revealed
Hunters spreading out to cover more regions

summary.title: Dusk — Transition to Night
bullets: Time marker advances from Monday day to Monday night
No revealed Dracula = no dusk combat
Each hunter now takes a night action

summary.title: Lord Godalming's Night Action: Supply
bullets: Bucharest is a large city — draws 1 item card
Bottom event card has Dracula icon — Dracula draws it instead
Night supply risk: Dracula may get the event card

summary.title: Dr. Seward's Night Action: The Mistake
bullets: Seward rests with no damage — recovers nothing
Completely wasted action
Should have supplied, reserved a ticket, or moved instead

summary.title: Lesson: Never Waste an Action
bullets: Every action counts — hunters are racing the clock
Dracula gains influence each round vampires mature
Only rest when you have actual damage to recover
Use spare actions to supply, get tickets, or reposition

summary.title: Van Helsing's Night Action: Supply
bullets: Amsterdam is a large city — draws 1 item card
Bottom event card has hunter icon — Van Helsing keeps it
Now equipped with ticket, item, and event card

summary.title: Mina Harker's Night Action: Supply
bullets: Paris is a large city — draws 1 item and 1 event card
Mina is always weakened (Drained ability) — must reveal 1 item + 1 event
Revealed cards are visible to all players including Dracula

summary.title: The Dracula Phase Begins
bullets: Existing hideouts slide one space right on the trail
Dracula places a new face-down location card on trail space 1
Hunters observe card back — city or sea? City this time

summary.title: Dracula Places an Encounter Card
bullets: Encounter card placed face-down on trail space 1
Could be trap, vampire, fog, or other hazard
Dracula draws back up to 5 encounter cards if needed

summary.title: Mistake: Mina Moves to the Trail
bullets: Mina stumbles onto a trail location — Dracula reveals it
Dracula ambushes with Unnatural Fog, placing fog tokens
Mina is now trapped and cannot search or easily leave
She was not prepared with useful items

summary.title: Lesson: Prepare Before Approaching the Trail
bullets: Always carry items and event cards before searching for Dracula
Encounters can trap, weaken, or redirect hunters
Coordinate approaches from multiple directions
Do not charge in unprepared

summary.title: Mistake: Hunters Ignore the Clock
bullets: Two despair tokens placed — vampire matures, influence jumps to 7
Hunters wandered gathering supplies without coordinating search
Halfway to losing without finding Dracula's region

summary.title: Lesson: Always Track the Clock
bullets: Time track and despair tokens are your urgency indicators
Spread out early to cover different regions
Use Mina's Psychic Bond to check regions
Communicate and converge when trail is found

summary.title: The Hunters Converge
bullets: Van Helsing uses railway to cross the map quickly
Lord Godalming stocks up on items with Wealth ability
Dr. Seward blocks escape routes
Open communication narrows Dracula's location

summary.title: First Combat with Dracula
bullets: Hunter moves into Dracula's city — Dracula is revealed
Combat at dusk: hunter takes Dodge/Punch/Escape, Dracula draws 5 combat cards
Round 1: Punch vs Claws — no icon match, both effects resolve
Damage dealt to both sides — the fight is on

summary.title: Continue the Round
bullets: Rounds repeat: Hunter Phase (day + night) then Dracula Phase
Hunters narrow location, equip, and converge
Dracula evades, plants vampires, and runs the clock
Play until one side wins

summary.title: Congratulations
bullets: You have seen the core game rhythm
Learned from 3 mistakes: wasting actions, approaching unprepared, ignoring the clock
Every action counts, always carry supplies, track influence
You are ready for a full game

summary.title: Before You Begin the Hunt
bullets: Check out General Tips for beginner strategy on both sides
Reference the Appendix for encounter cards, combat cards, and terms
Ask your personal GM Guide — tap Q&A for instant answers during play

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Before diving into strategy, here are the biggest beginner traps. First, hunters bunching up together and leaving entire regions unsearched. Second, wasting actions on rest when you have no damage. Third, Dracula moving by sea too aggressively and losing too much health. Fourth, hunters ignoring the time track and being surprised by despair tokens and maturing vampires. Fifth, forgetting character abilities like Mina's Psychic Bond or Lord Godalming's Wealth. Keep these in mind and your first game will go much more smoothly.

walkthrough.title: Hunter Strategy: Spread Out Early
walkthrough.text: In the early game, the hunters' biggest challenge is that Dracula could be almost anywhere in Europe. The best approach is to spread out and cover as many regions as possible. Each hunter should move toward a different area of the map. As you move, you will check locations against Dracula's trail, narrowing down where he could be. Avoid moving in the same direction as another hunter. Four hunters covering four regions are far more effective than four hunters in the same city.

walkthrough.title: Hunter Strategy: Supply in Large Cities
walkthrough.text: Whenever you pass through a large city and do not have urgent movement needs, take a supply action. Item cards are critical for combat, and event cards can give you powerful advantages. Lord Godalming should supply as often as possible since his Wealth ability lets him draw two item cards instead of one. During night supply, be aware that Dracula might get the event card instead of you if the bottom card has a Dracula icon. Balance supply runs with movement to avoid falling behind on the search.

walkthrough.title: Hunter Strategy: Use Tickets Wisely
walkthrough.text: Railway tickets are extremely valuable because they let you cross vast distances in a single action. Reserve tickets during night actions when you have nothing urgent to do. Save your best tickets for when you find the trail and need to converge quickly. Lord Godalming gets two tickets per reserve action, making him the team's fastest responder. Do not waste high-value tickets on short trips. A ticket with a 3 or 4 value can let you race across half the continent in one move.

walkthrough.title: Dracula Strategy: Stay Inland Early
walkthrough.text: Sea movement is tempting for Dracula because it lets him cross large distances, but it comes with serious costs. Moving from port to sea costs two damage, and sea to sea costs one damage. The different card back on sea location cards also alerts the hunters. In the early game, stay inland and use road movement to build a long trail. Save sea movement for emergency escapes when hunters are closing in. Castle Dracula is a great mid-game destination because it heals five damage.

walkthrough.title: Dracula Strategy: Place Vampires Early
walkthrough.text: Your primary win condition is advancing the influence track to 13 through maturing vampire encounters. Place vampire encounter cards on the trail as early as possible so they have the most time to mature. A vampire on trail space one needs six Dracula phases to mature. If you wait too long to place vampires, the hunters will find your trail before they can mature. Mix in non-vampire encounters like fog and bats to slow down hunters who find your trail.

walkthrough.title: Communication and Coordination
walkthrough.text: Hunters must communicate openly. All table talk must happen in the presence of Dracula. Share information about which cities you have checked, what items and events you have, and where you think Dracula might be. Use the process of elimination to narrow down Dracula's region. If three regions are clear, Dracula must be in the fourth. The only private communication happens during a trade action, where two hunters in the same city can share cards without Dracula seeing or hearing.

walkthrough.title: Understand Character Abilities
walkthrough.text: Each character has unique abilities that shape their role. Lord Godalming has Wealth, drawing two items when supplying in large cities, and Privilege, gaining two tickets per reserve action. He is the team's quartermaster and fastest mover. Dr. Seward has Physician, recovering two damage per rest, and Prepared, holding up to four cards of each type. He is the team's medic and pack mule. Van Helsing has Leader, allowing him to trade event cards with any hunter anywhere, and Strong Willed, giving him two bite spaces. He is the team's coordinator. Mina Harker has Psychic Bond, forcing Dracula to reveal if he is in her region, but Drained means she always has one bite and must reveal cards. She is the team's tracker.

summary.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
bullets: Hunters bunching up and leaving regions unsearched
Wasting actions (resting with no damage)
Dracula moving by sea too aggressively (costs health, reveals card back)
Ignoring the time track and being surprised by despair/maturing vampires
Forgetting character abilities

summary.title: Hunter Strategy: Spread Out Early
bullets: Each hunter moves toward a different region of Europe
Moving through cities checks them against Dracula's trail
Four hunters in four regions >> four hunters in one city
Narrow down Dracula's location by elimination

summary.title: Hunter Strategy: Supply in Large Cities
bullets: Take supply actions whenever passing through a large city
Items are critical for combat; events provide powerful advantages
Lord Godalming draws 2 items (Wealth) — supply often
Night supply risk: Dracula may get the event card

summary.title: Hunter Strategy: Use Tickets Wisely
bullets: Reserve tickets during low-priority night actions
Save high-value tickets (3-4) for convergence when trail is found
Lord Godalming gets 2 tickets per reserve action (Privilege)
Do not waste high-value tickets on short trips

summary.title: Dracula Strategy: Stay Inland Early
bullets: Sea movement costs 2 damage (port to sea) or 1 damage (sea to sea)
Sea location cards have different card back — alerts hunters
Stay inland and build a long trail early
Save sea for emergency escapes; Castle Dracula heals 5 damage

summary.title: Dracula Strategy: Place Vampires Early
bullets: Vampire encounters are your primary win condition
Place vampires early — they need 6 Dracula phases to mature
Mix in fog, bats, and traps to slow hunters on your trail
Waiting too long = hunters find trail before vampires mature

summary.title: Communication and Coordination
bullets: All hunter communication must be open (Dracula hears everything)
Share info on checked cities, items, and suspected Dracula regions
Use elimination: if 3 regions are clear, Dracula is in the 4th
Only trade actions allow private communication between two hunters

summary.title: Understand Character Abilities
bullets: Lord Godalming: Wealth (2 items) + Privilege (2 tickets) — quartermaster
Dr. Seward: Physician (rest heals 2) + Prepared (hand limit 4) — medic
Van Helsing: Leader (trade events anywhere) + Strong Willed (2 bite spaces) — coordinator
Mina Harker: Psychic Bond (check Dracula's region) + Drained (always weakened) — tracker

walkthrough.title: Hunter Net Strategy
walkthrough.text: The most effective hunter strategy is the shrinking net. In the early game, spread the four hunters to cover four different regions of Europe. As you eliminate regions where Dracula cannot be, converge inward. The ideal formation puts hunters on multiple sides of Dracula's suspected region, cutting off escape routes. When one hunter finds the trail, the other three should immediately start moving to surround that area rather than all rushing to the same location.

walkthrough.title: Exploiting Mina's Psychic Bond
walkthrough.text: Mina Harker's Psychic Bond is the single most powerful tracking tool in the game. As a special action, if Mina is in the same city as another hunter, she can force Dracula to reveal whether he is in her region. This effectively clears or confirms an entire region of the map in one action. The optimal play is to have Mina systematically check regions by moving to key cities and using Psychic Bond. Pair her with another hunter as a travel companion since she needs a hunter in her city to use it. This can narrow down Dracula's location extremely fast.

walkthrough.title: Combat Preparation and Card Selection
walkthrough.text: Combat is the decisive moment of the game. Hunters should enter combat with as many item cards as possible. Weapon cards like Pistol and Rifle deal significant damage to Dracula. Crucifix is powerful at night. The key combat principle is understanding the icon matching system. Each hunter combat card and item card has highlighted icons on its banner. If one of those icons matches the icon on Dracula's revealed combat card, Dracula's effect is canceled. Dodge has the most highlighted icons and is the safest defensive play. Punch and weapon cards are offensive but riskier.

walkthrough.title: Multi-Hunter Combat Tactics
walkthrough.text: When multiple hunters are in Dracula's city, combat becomes significantly more favorable. Dracula must choose one engaged hunter whose card can potentially block his effect, but both hunters' cards resolve. This means you get double the damage output while Dracula can only target one hunter at a time. Ideally, the engaged hunter plays defensively with Dodge or a card with many blocking icons, while the other hunter plays an offensive weapon card. This forces Dracula into a lose-lose: either his card is blocked, or both hunters' effects still resolve.

walkthrough.title: Dracula's Trail Management
walkthrough.text: As Dracula, your trail is both your path and your weapon. Each of the six trail spaces is a potential vampire incubator or hunter trap. The optimal approach is to layer encounters strategically. Place vampire encounters early when they have the most time to mature. Place defensive encounters like Unnatural Fog or Bats on spaces you expect hunters to find. Remember that you cannot place a location card that is already on the trail, which constrains your movement. Plan your route several turns ahead to avoid dead ends where you would be forced to backtrack through revealed locations.

walkthrough.title: Using Power Cards Effectively
walkthrough.text: In the advanced game, Dracula's five power cards are game-changing tools. Hide is the most versatile: it lets you stay in place while appearing to move, and it places an encounter card. Use Hide when hunters are far away and you want to stack encounters without changing location. Wolf Form lets you move two cities by road for one damage, which is excellent for escaping a closing net. Feed recovers up to three damage and is essential after sea travel or combat. Dark Call is a high-risk investment at two damage, but drawing five encounter cards dramatically increases your chances of getting vampire encounters. Misdirect clears a hideout and can remove a revealed location to mislead hunters.

walkthrough.title: Lair Strategy for Dracula
walkthrough.text: Lairs are advanced tools that let Dracula maintain more encounter-bearing locations than the six trail spaces allow. When a hideout would slide off the trail, you can convert it to a lair instead of maturing it. This is powerful because you can place an additional encounter card on the lair, and you can later return to a lair by placing it back on the trail. The best use of lairs is to park vampires that are not yet ready to mature in a safe lair slot while you continue building your trail. You can also use lairs to store defensive encounters near your intended travel route.

walkthrough.title: Rumor Tokens and Influence Spikes
walkthrough.text: Rumor tokens are placed on hideouts in the first three trail spaces after Dracula places an encounter card. When a vampire encounter matures from a hideout that has a rumor token, the influence track advances by three additional spaces. This means a single maturing vampire with a rumor token can advance influence by the card's value plus three. Combined with Dracula's Fury of Dracula ability after all three despair tokens are placed, you can spike the influence track dramatically in a single turn. Time your vampire maturations to coincide with rumor tokens and the Fury ability for maximum impact.

walkthrough.title: Hunter Endgame: The Final Push
walkthrough.text: In the late game, when despair tokens are building and Dracula's influence is climbing, the hunters must shift from searching to attacking. If you know Dracula's approximate location, converge multiple hunters to the same city. Do not spread out anymore. Two or three hunters in combat with Dracula can deal massive damage in a few rounds. Make sure at least one hunter has strong weapon cards. Use event cards aggressively. If Dracula is near 13 influence, you cannot afford to play conservatively. The final push should be an all-out assault.

walkthrough.title: Dracula Endgame: Running Out the Clock
walkthrough.text: When Dracula's influence is high and despair tokens are stacking, your goal shifts to pure survival. Avoid combat at all costs. Use Wolf Form to make large jumps away from hunter clusters. Use Hide to buy time while vampires mature. Sea movement is now worth the damage cost if it creates distance. Place Unnatural Fog and roadblock encounters to slow pursuing hunters. With the Fury of Dracula ability active, every turn you place on the trail gains three influence. You only need a few more turns. Keep moving, keep placing encounters, and let the clock do the work.

summary.title: Hunter Net Strategy
bullets: Spread 4 hunters to cover 4 regions in early game
Converge inward as regions are eliminated
When trail is found, surround the area — don't all rush to one spot
Cut off escape routes on multiple sides

summary.title: Exploiting Mina's Psychic Bond
bullets: Psychic Bond clears/confirms an entire region in one action
Requires another hunter in Mina's city
Systematically check regions to narrow Dracula's location fast
Pair Mina with a travel companion for maximum efficiency

summary.title: Combat Preparation and Card Selection
bullets: Enter combat with maximum item cards (weapons deal heavy damage)
Icon matching: if hunter banner icon matches Dracula's card icon, his effect is canceled
Dodge = safest (most blocking icons), weapons = offensive but riskier
Crucifix is powerful at night

summary.title: Multi-Hunter Combat Tactics
bullets: Multiple hunters = double damage output, Dracula can only target one
Engaged hunter plays Dodge (defensive), other plays weapon (offensive)
Forces Dracula into lose-lose situations
Converge 2-3 hunters for decisive combat

summary.title: Dracula's Trail Management
bullets: 6 trail spaces = 6 potential vampire incubators or traps
Place vampires early (need time to mature), traps where hunters approach
Cannot repeat a location already on trail — plan routes ahead
Avoid dead ends that force backtracking through revealed locations

summary.title: Using Power Cards Effectively
bullets: Hide: stay in place + place encounter (best when hunters are far away)
Wolf Form: move 2 cities for 1 damage (escape closing nets)
Feed: recover 3 damage (essential after sea/combat). Dark Call: 2 damage for 5 encounters
Misdirect: clear a hideout to mislead hunters about your trail

summary.title: Lair Strategy for Dracula
bullets: Convert maturing hideouts to lairs instead of resolving them
Park not-yet-ready vampires safely in lair slots (up to 3 lairs)
Return to a lair later by placing it back on the trail
Stack additional encounter cards on lairs for ambush potential

summary.title: Rumor Tokens and Influence Spikes
bullets: Place rumor tokens on hideouts in trail spaces 1-3
Maturing vampire with rumor token = card value + 3 bonus influence
Time vampire maturations with rumor tokens and Fury ability
Single turn can spike influence dramatically

summary.title: Hunter Endgame: The Final Push
bullets: Late game: shift from searching to attacking
Converge 2-3 hunters to Dracula's city for decisive combat
Ensure at least one hunter has strong weapon cards
Use event cards aggressively — no more conservative play

summary.title: Dracula Endgame: Running Out the Clock
bullets: High influence: avoid combat at all costs
Wolf Form and sea movement for maximum distance from hunters
Hide buys time while vampires mature
Fury of Dracula active = +3 influence per trail placement, just survive

entries.definition: The main victory track for Dracula, running from 0 to 13. Dracula wins when it reaches 13. Advanced by maturing vampires, defeating hunters, resolving Fangs against mesmerized hunters, and the Fury of Dracula ability.

A row of six spaces on the board where Dracula places face-down location cards to secretly track his movement. The first space is his current location. Cards slide right each Dracula phase. When a sixth card would slide off, that hideout matures.

All cards and tokens on one trail space, typically consisting of a location card with an encounter card on top. Hideouts slide along the trail each Dracula phase and mature when pushed off the sixth space.

An advanced-rules feature. Instead of maturing a hideout sliding off the trail, Dracula can convert it to a lair by placing it on one of three lair slots on the board. Lairs can hold multiple encounter cards and can be placed back on the trail later.

Placed on the time track each time a full week passes (Sunday night to Monday day). There are three despair token spaces. Despair tokens increase influence gained from defeating hunters and affect Dracula's Pride ability. After all three are placed, the Fury of Dracula ability activates.

Tracks the passage of days and nights throughout the game. Alternates between day and night spaces through the days of the week. Advances at dawn (night to day) and dusk (day to night). A full cycle triggers a despair token placement.

When a hunter moves to Dracula's current location (trail space 1), Dracula is revealed and his figure is placed on the board. Combat occurs at the next dawn or dusk. Dracula can also be revealed when hunters find locations on his trail. Dracula cannot be revealed while on a sea zone.

The effect printed at the bottom of some encounter cards in a red section. Resolved when a hideout containing that encounter card slides off the sixth trail space or when a lair's encounter cards are matured by Dracula. Vampire matured effects advance the influence track.

The first phase of each round. Contains dawn, day actions, dusk, and night actions. Each hunter performs one action during day and one during night, in activation order. Dawn and dusk include time advancement and potential combat with a revealed Dracula.

The second phase of each round. Dracula slides trail cards, places a new location card face-down on trail space 1, then places an encounter card. In the advanced game, Dracula may also place a rumor token and manage lairs.
Occurs at the start of the hunter phase. The time marker advances from a night space to a day space. If Dracula is revealed and in a hunter's city, combat occurs at dawn.

Occurs between day and night actions. The time marker advances from a day space to a night space. If Dracula is revealed and in a hunter's city, combat occurs at dusk. At the end of dusk, one fog, storm, and roadblock token is removed from the board.
The number printed on the upper-left corner of each character sheet. Determines the order in which hunters perform their day and night actions.

A hunter action that allows travel by road, railway, or sea. Road movement goes to an adjacent city. Railway movement requires spending a ticket token. Sea movement is day only and goes from a port to an adjacent sea zone or vice versa.

A hunter action to draw cards. In a large city, draw 1 item card (Lord Godalming draws 2). Then draw an event card: top of deck during day, bottom during night. Hunter-icon events go to the hunter; Dracula-icon events are discarded (day) or go to Dracula (night).
A hunter action to reveal and resolve all encounter cards at the hunter's current location on the trail. Ignores matured effects. Cannot be performed in a city with a fog token.
A hunter action to recover 1 damage (Dr. Seward recovers 2 due to Physician ability). Only useful when the hunter has damage tokens on their character sheet.

A hunter action to exchange item cards and ticket tokens with another hunter in the same city. This exchange is private — Dracula cannot see the cards or hear the conversation. Van Helsing can trade event cards with a hunter in any city.
A hunter action to gain 1 ticket token from the pool (Lord Godalming gains 2 due to Privilege ability). Each hunter can hold up to 2 ticket tokens. Ticket tokens are used for railway movement.
A hunter action to use an ability from a character sheet or an event card that specifies it can be used as an action. Mina's Psychic Bond is a notable special action.

A hunter character. Wealth ability: draw 2 item cards when supplying in a large city. Privilege ability: gain 2 ticket tokens per reserve action. Starts in Constanta. The team's quartermaster and fastest mover.
A hunter character. Physician ability: rest recovers 2 damage instead of 1. Prepared ability: hand limit of 4 for each card type instead of 3. Starts in Marseilles. The team's medic and pack mule.

A hunter character. Leader ability: can trade event cards (instead of items) with a hunter in any city. Strong Willed ability: has 2 bite spaces (defeated on 3rd bite). Starts in Amsterdam. The team's coordinator.

A hunter character. Psychic Bond ability: as a special action in the same city as another hunter, force Dracula to reveal if he is in her region. Drained ability: always has 1 bite token and is always weakened (must reveal 1 item + 1 event card). Starts in Brussels. Defeated on first additional bite.

The antagonist controlled by one player. Moves secretly by placing location cards on the trail. Places encounter cards to create vampires and traps. Wins by advancing influence to 13. Has Rage, Pride, and Fury of Dracula abilities on his character sheet.
After a hunter is defeated, advance the influence track by 2 plus the number of despair tokens on the time track. Makes defeating hunters increasingly rewarding for Dracula as the game progresses.

If Dracula plays Escape as Mist or Escape as Bat before he has played a number of combat cards greater than the number of despair tokens on the time track, the escape card is canceled. Forces Dracula to fight before fleeing.

After Dracula places a city location card or Hide on the trail while all 3 despair tokens are on the time track, advance the influence track by 3. This late-game ability creates extreme urgency for the hunters.

An encounter card. When resolved by search or ambush, the hunter must fight this vampire. When matured, advances the influence track and clears specific hideouts. One of Dracula's primary victory tools.
An encounter card. A stronger vampire encounter. When matured, advances the influence track by a higher value than New Vampire. Must be fought if encountered by a searching hunter.

An encounter card. Another vampire variant. When matured, advances the influence track. Must be fought if encountered by a searching hunter. Each vampire type has different health and matured effect values.
An encounter card. Places fog tokens on the city. When matured, can place 2 fog tokens on a city in the region. Fog prevents search actions and blocks movement through the affected city.

When a hunter ends movement on a hideout with encounter cards, Dracula may choose to reveal and resolve one encounter card against that hunter, ignoring the matured effect. The card is then discarded. Dracula can decline to ambush.
A Dracula combat card. The engaged hunter suffers 2 damage. At night, choose any hunter in this combat to suffer 2 additional damage. One of Dracula's most dangerous combat cards.
A Dracula combat card. The engaged hunter suffers 2 damage. If the hunter played Punch, Crucifix, or a weapon card, cancel that card. Powerful against offensive hunter plays.
A Dracula combat card. The engaged hunter is bitten. If the hunter is mesmerized, advance the influence track by 1. Biting is extremely dangerous as it weakens hunters and can defeat them.
A Dracula combat card. Ends the combat immediately. Subject to the Pride ability restriction: Dracula must have played enough combat cards (more than despair tokens) before this resolves.

A Dracula combat card. Ends the combat. If it is night and Dracula was in the combat, Dracula may clear his current hideout and secretly choose a city up to 2 roads away that is not occupied by a hunter. Subject to Pride restriction.

A Dracula combat card. The engaged hunter cannot use item or event cards for the remainder of this combat. Makes the hunter vulnerable to Fangs, which gains bonus influence against mesmerized hunters.

A hunter combat card with a yellow banner. Has the most highlighted blocking icons. When played, the hunter is not affected by Dracula's card if the icon matches. The safest defensive option in combat. No offensive effect.
A hunter combat card with a yellow banner. Dracula (or the vampire) suffers 1 damage. A basic offensive option available every combat.
A hunter combat card with a yellow banner. The hunter is removed from the combat. Used when a hunter is outmatched or low on health and needs to flee.
A hunter item card with a weapon banner. The vampire suffers 2 damage. A strong offensive combat card.
A hunter item card with a weapon banner. Deals damage to Dracula or vampires in combat. One of the hunters' best offensive tools.
A hunter item card. Effective in combat, especially at night. Can cancel certain Dracula combat cards. A key defensive and offensive tool against vampires.
A hunter combat card. Choose 1 or more hunters to be removed from this combat. Then discard this card. Used to protect multiple hunters from a dangerous situation.
Dracula places this on the trail instead of moving. Does not place an encounter card. Recovers up to 3 damage. Essential for healing after sea travel or combat damage.

Dracula places this on the trail instead of moving. Does not place an encounter card. Suffers 2 damage but draws 5 encounter cards. No hand limit for encounter cards. High-risk, high-reward for finding vampires.

Dracula places this on the trail along with a face-down location card. Suffers 1 damage. The location card can be up to 2 cities away by road, passing undetected through adjacent cities with hunters. Excellent for escaping.

Dracula chooses a hideout on the trail and clears it. Then places Misdirect on that cleared space. After, Dracula places a card on trail space 1 as normal. Cannot clear hideouts containing power cards, Castle Dracula, current location, or Hide-associated locations.

Placed face-down on the trail with the same card back as location cards. Dracula stays in his current location. An encounter card is placed on it, but the matured effect of that encounter cannot be resolved. If the adjacent location is revealed, Hide is also revealed.

Placed on a city by encounter or event cards. Prevents search actions in that city. Hunters cannot move into, out of, or through a city with a fog token. Dracula may avoid combat in a fogged city. One token removed per city at each dusk.

Placed under a hunter figure. Dracula can move that hunter to an adjacent city as the hunter's next action. After moving the hunter, the token is returned to the pool. A disruptive tool that wastes a hunter's action.
Placed on a sea zone. Hunters cannot enter that sea zone. One storm token is removed at the end of each dusk. Used to block hunter sea travel routes.
Placed on a road or railway connection. Hunters cannot move through that connection. Double-sided with a road side and a rail side. One token is removed at the end of each dusk.

Placed on a location. If on the trail, that location and its encounters are revealed. Dracula cannot enter that location. If it is Dracula's current location, he must move away next Dracula phase. Permanent until removed by game effects.
Functions the same as consecrated ground but is removed at dawn. Reveals the location and encounters, blocks Dracula from entering. Three available in the token pool.

Placed on hideouts in the first three trail spaces after Dracula places an encounter card. Dracula starts with 1 and gains 1 each time a despair token is placed. When a vampire matures from a hideout with a rumor token, influence advances by 3 additional spaces.

Placed on a hunter's bite space when bitten. A hunter with one or more bite tokens is weakened and must keep 1 item card and 1 event card revealed. Most hunters have 1 bite space (defeated on 2nd bite). Van Helsing has 2 bite spaces. Mina starts with 1 permanent bite.

Placed on character sheets when damage is suffered. Come in values of 1 and 5. When total damage equals or exceeds health value, the character is defeated. Removed by rest actions or healing effects. Dracula is defeated at 15 damage.

Gained by the Reserve a Ticket action. Has a white value and sometimes a yellow value. Spent to move by railway. White value for white-only railways, yellow value for any combination involving yellow railways. Each hunter can hold up to 2 tickets.
A city on the board where hunters can draw item cards when supplying. Identified by a larger circle on the map. Key destinations for supply actions.
A city on the board where hunters cannot draw item cards when supplying (only event cards). Identified by a smaller circle. Still useful for movement and searching.
A city adjacent to a sea zone, identified by an anchor icon. Hunters and Dracula can move between a port and its adjacent sea zone. Dracula suffers 2 damage when moving from a port to sea.

A non-city location on the board representing open water. Divided by borders; adjacent sea zones share a border. Hunters must move during day and pass during night while at sea. Dracula suffers 1 damage moving between sea zones.

A special location with a unique card back. When Dracula places this on the trail, he heals 5 damage. Hunters at Castle Dracula cannot supply or reserve tickets. Its distinctive card back alerts hunters when it is played.

Special locations attached to Madrid, Rome, and Budapest. Defeated hunters are placed at the nearest hospital (by road) at dawn. At a hospital, hunters can only supply for item cards and can move to the attached city during day.
A connection between two adjacent cities on the board. Hunters and Dracula can move along roads. The most basic form of movement, requiring no tokens or special abilities.
A connection between cities that hunters can use with ticket tokens. Comes in white and yellow varieties. Allows movement over greater distances than roads. Dracula cannot use railways.
A section of the European map. Used by Mina's Psychic Bond ability to determine if Dracula is in a particular area. Helps hunters narrow down Dracula's location systematically.

A hunter state caused by having one or more bite tokens. A weakened hunter must keep 1 item card and 1 event card revealed (face-up) at all times. These revealed cards are visible to all players including Dracula but are still hidden during combat.

A hunter is defeated when damage tokens equal or exceed their health, or when they receive a bite with no empty bite spaces. The hunter discards all cards, returns all tokens, and is placed at the nearest hospital at dawn. Influence advances by 2 + despair tokens.
Dracula is defeated when he has 15 total damage on his character sheet. The hunters immediately win the game.

Some event cards can be played as allies. Hunter allies are placed in the hunter ally slot on the board; Dracula allies are placed face-up in Dracula's play area. Only 1 hunter ally and 1 Dracula ally can exist at a time. New allies replace old ones.

Cards with timing text that provide powerful effects for hunters or Dracula. Drawn during supply actions. Some must be played immediately, others saved. Hunter and Dracula event cards are shuffled into one deck. 75 total: 50 hunter, 25 Dracula.
Cards providing hunters with weapons, tools, and equipment. Most are used in combat (have a colored banner). Drawn during supply actions in large cities. 38 total in the item deck.

Cards representing each location on the board, used by Dracula to secretly track his movement on the trail. 70 total: 60 land and 10 sea. Sea cards have a different back. Organized numerically in the location deck.

Cards placed face-down on the trail by Dracula. Represent traps, vampires, and hazards. Resolved when hunters search or when Dracula ambushes. Some have matured effects. Dracula maintains a hand of 5. 28 total in the encounter deck.

Cards used by Dracula during combat. 13 total, shuffled into a deck. Dracula draws 5 at the start of combat and 1 more each round. Include Claws, Strength, Fangs, Mesmerize, Escape as Mist, and Escape as Bat.

Three standard cards (Dodge, Punch, Escape) taken from shared piles at the start of combat. Added to the hunter's hand alongside item cards. Do not count toward hand limits. Returned to piles after combat ends.

All hunter communication must be open and in Dracula's presence. Hunters can share information about cards in their hands, but must show cards to Dracula as well. The only private communication occurs during a trade action between two hunters in the same city.

Most hunters can hold 3 item cards and 3 event cards. Dr. Seward can hold 4 of each (Prepared ability). Dracula can hold 4 event cards. There is no hand limit for Dracula's encounter cards. Excess cards must be discarded.

During combat, the icon on Dracula's revealed card is compared to highlighted icons on the hunter's card banner. If they match, Dracula's effect is canceled. This is the hunter's primary defense mechanism in combat.

In multi-hunter combat, Dracula chooses one engaged hunter before cards are revealed. Only the engaged hunter's card can potentially block Dracula's combat card through icon matching. Both hunters' card effects still resolve.
